behavior_summary: Provides an examine/U8P_DEADNS::look handler that shows the short description 'dead body' when the player inspects the item.

Provides a U8P_DEADNS::use handler for the dead-body item that reads the item's status flags and branches: if a particular status bit is set it closes the item's gump and aborts; otherwise it performs a rare random check to produce a bark and suspend, falling back to opening the item's gump UI.

The script uses per-item status bits (read via Item::getStatus) to gate interaction, a random roll (urandom(100) > 95) to occasionally block/open a bark, and Item::openGump(0x0C, this) as the normal UI path.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DEADNS::look: single linear path — emit bark/description 'dead body' then suspend/end the U8P_DEADNS::look process.
U8P_DEADNS::use: read item status into a temporary (local1). If the tested status bit (inferred 0x0100) is set -> call Item::closeGump(this) and stop.
U8P_DEADNS::use: if that bit is clear -> evaluate urandom(100) > 95. On a rare true result (~4% inferred) -> bark 'leave my bones alone...' and suspend; otherwise -> call Item::openGump(0x0C, this).
open_questions: What exact per-item status bit is being tested (decompilation suggests 0x0100 but the precise meaning is unknown)?
What game-side semantics does the 'suspend' node implement here (temporary pause, prevent openGump, or block repeated immediate actions)?
Are there other scripts or game-wide flags that alter the meaning of the tested status bit or the gump id 0x0C for this item?

main_function.name: U8P_DEADNS::look
main_function.purpose: Display a short description when the player examines the dead-body item.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player 'looks at' or inspects the object (U8P_DEADNS::look/examine process).
Treated as an area-entry/examine trigger (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_DEADNS::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_DEADNS::look handler that emits the short description 'dead body' and ends the U8P_DEADNS::look process

function_map.name: U8P_DEADNS::use
function_map.role: handle player 'U8P_DEADNS::use' interaction: check per-item status, occasionally bark/suspend, otherwise open the item's gump UI

external_calls.purpose: Read per-item status/flags into a temporary to decide branching (test specific bit inferred to be 0x0100).
Close any open UI/gump for this item when the status bit indicates interaction should be aborted.
Generate a random number in range 0..99 to implement a rare (~4%) branch that barks and suspends instead of opening the gump.
Open the item's UI/gump (gump id 0x0C) in the normal (non-blocked, non-rare) path.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides an examine/U8P_DEADNS::look handler that shows the short description 'dead body' when the player inspects the item.
